authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes the model retail food code used by U.S. retail food regulators. It is the primary public baseline for operational controls, contamination prevention, consumer advisories, and managed food-service execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service readiness, in-process food handling, and completion-stop conditions
used_for: evidence expectations for contamination and allergen-sensitive work

authoritative_sources.title: Retail Food Protection Employee Health and Personal Hygiene Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's employee health handbook is an authoritative public operating reference for employee illness reporting, restriction and exclusion decisions, and hygiene expectations in restaurant service environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: shift readiness, service interruption, and employee-health escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Menu Labeling Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes the primary federal guidance for chain-restaurant menu labeling. It is authoritative for guest-facing menu disclosure boundaries and helps keep the lane from inventing or altering regulated menu representations.
used_for: escalation triggers for inaccurate menu representations

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator explaining billing-error timelines and customer rights. It is authoritative for packaging duplicate-charge, charge-error, and refund-not-posted handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, refund-posting review, and merchant-versus-issuer escalation

authoritative_sources.title: California's Hidden Fees Statute (SB 478)
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's Attorney General publishes the enforcement-facing guidance for all-in advertised pricing and mandatory-fee disclosure under SB 478. It is authoritative for jurisdiction-sensitive restaurant service-fee and advertised-price boundary handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: guest-check pricing review and fee-disclosure conflict handling

authoritative_sources.title: Tax Guide for Restaurant Owners: Mandatory Charges
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Tax and Fee Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDTFA publishes the public operating reference for how restaurant mandatory charges are characterized, including banquet service charges and related fees. It directly informs fee-handling terminology and evidence boundaries in restaurant guest checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: banquet check review, service-charge handling, and guest-check evidence packaging

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Security Standards Council Publishes Merchant Guidance on Skimming Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card security. Its merchant skimming-prevention guidance is authoritative for restaurant payment-device tampering awareness and boundary handling when suspicious tender activity affects service execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-device anomaly handling and loss-prevention escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Food Defense Self-Assessment Tool for Retail Food Stores and Food Service Establishments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's retail food defense tool is an authoritative public reference for restricted access, suspicious-activity review, intentional adulteration awareness, and tampering-sensitive operational controls in food service establishments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: tampering response, suspicious activity review, and service-stop escalation

workflow_stages.name: Reservation, intake, and service-readiness grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant, location, service model, reservation or order identifier, party context, current lifecycle state, and named systems of record before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope or blocked.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Order, menu, and payment prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify menu and modifier truth, allergen notes, booking deposits, guest-check linkage, split-tender posture, promotions, mandatory charges, and service-ticket dependencies so execution does not proceed on stale or incomplete records.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: In-process service execution and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track seated, in-prep, fired, delivered, rework, recovered, blocked, and ready-to-close states while preserving payment, pricing, and guest-impact boundaries.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Safety, tampering, and loss-prevention stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve food-safety, employee-health, allergen, contamination, tampering, and suspicious payment-device boundaries whenever a case carries customer harm or fraud implications.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence, refund boundary, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when service, payment, refund, recovery, and notification evidence exists and any tenant, franchise, marketplace, or jurisdiction rules stricter than the public baseline have been applied.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: reservations, waitlist entries, guest profiles, party-size notes, and seating assignments
POS guest checks, PMS or booking events, order tickets, modifier records, and service tickets
payment ledgers, split-tender details, reservation deposits, refund logs, void notes, and promotion records
menu configurations, allergen notes, service-recovery notes, remake tickets, and guest communication records
inventory states, item availability records, food-safety logs, employee illness or exclusion notes, and contamination reports
loss-prevention holds, payment-device review notes, suspicious-activity records, and closure or signoff artifacts

allowed: Classify restaurant work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, on hold, rework, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented reservation, order, payment, and service state.
Prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required booking, order, payment, safety, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, fee-waiver approval, legal interpretation, fraud decisioning, or food-safety signoff.
A public baseline and tenant, franchise, marketplace, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
An allergen, contamination, tampering, suspicious device, or customer-harm condition changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of service completion, settlement or approved disposition, and required guest-recovery or incident records.
Override pricing, refund, fee-disclosure, or safety policies without explicit authority.
Continue normal service execution after unresolved food-safety, employee-health, tampering, or fraud stop conditions appear.

domain_failure_modes: a reservation, order, or payment case is closed even though POS, booking, and payment records conflict
a refund or service-fee waiver is granted without delegated authority or jurisdiction-aware policy review
service proceeds despite unresolved allergen, contamination, or employee-health stop conditions
a suspected skimming or tampering signal is treated as ordinary guest-service friction and the case is completed anyway
a banquet or large-party check is explained or altered without the required fee-disclosure and mandatory-charge context
completion is declared without payment settlement, remake closure, guest acknowledgment, or incident documentation
restaurant operations drift into generic customer service instead of reservation, ticket, check, safety, and throughput-sensitive execution

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific service-charge, automatic-gratuity, reservation-deposit, and no-show rules vary materially across states, cities, and franchise systems.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FDA, CFPB, California, and PCI SSC sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, tenant posting practices, marketplace terms, and franchise policy facts before applying final pricing or refund outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Hotel, casino, venue, and concession restaurants can inherit adjacent folio, resort-fee, or event-billing systems that change the guest-check authority surface.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline remains usable, but tenant-specific PMS, folio, event-contract, and signoff overlays must be supplied at runtime before those specialized outcomes are finalized.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, payment handling, pricing transparency, food-safety stop conditions, and tampering or skimming-sensitive failure behavior instead of only guest-service policy.

The lane stops at restaurant execution. Final pricing decisions, refund or fee waivers, fraud findings, legal interpretation, and food-safety signoff stay outside the lane even when guest pressure or service-speed pressure is high.
